figures.caption: Fig. 1. Flow chart of the proposed kinodynamic planner: A terrain-adaptive ϵ-constraint optimization is used to find the sequence of controls with that best satisfies both objectives (f1, f2) while ensuring all the controls in the sequence are from the space of valid controls.

Fig. 2. Comparison of nominal model, corrected model, and ground truth for robot pose estimation. While x, y, and yaw are well captured by the nominal model, roll and pitch predictions are (up to 32%) improved by the Gaussian process residual correction; z depends stochastically on terrain geometry.

Fig. 3. Vector field over a mesh guiding an agent toward the goal while avoiding lethal regions. The color gradient represents the scalar scaling function s(p) (s = 2), with intensity increasing near the goal.
